zh.Saffron Walden, Uttlesford district, Ess: 英國英格蘭艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登
zh.Saffron Walden, Essex, England: 英國英格蘭艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Sothy於1574年出生在英國英格蘭艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登時，他的父親Sir Thomas South29歲，母親Audrey Michell27歲。他於1595年與Mary Bentley在英國艾可德布登結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1626年12月在英國艾可德布登享年52歲，埋葬在英國艾可德布登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Sothy于1574年出生在英国英格兰艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登时，他的父亲Sir Thomas South29岁，母亲Audrey Michell27岁。他于1595年与Mary Bentley在英国艾可德布登结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1626年12月在英国艾可德布登享年52岁，埋葬在英国艾可德布登。

nameMeanings.name: Sothy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Him, Chan, Hin, Pok, Roeun, Sarin, Sin, Song.
North German (Söth) and Danish: nickname from Middle Low German sote ‘sweet’.

variants.name: Soder
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Thor, Erik, Hilmer, Sig.  German Erna, Ewald, Hans, Viktor.

German (also Söder): topographic name from Middle High German sōt ‘puddle, wet ground’, Middle Low German sod ‘turf’, the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant, or a habitational name for someone from Söder near Hildesheim, Soden (Hesse, Bavaria), or Sood (Baden-Württemberg).
German (also Söder): topographic name from Middle Low German, Middle High German sōt ‘spring, well, salt spring’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Sothy was born in 1574, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Thomas South, was 29 and his mother, Audrey Michell, was 27. He married Mary Bentley in 1595, in Debden, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in December 1626, in Debden, Essex, England, at the age of 52, and was buried in Debden, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Sothy est né en 1574, à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Thomas South, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Audrey Michell, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Mary Bentley en 1595, à Debden, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en décembre 1626, à Debden, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Debden, Essex, Angleterre.

ja.Saffron Walden, Essex, England: イギリスイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデン
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Sothyが1574年にイギリスイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Southは29歳，母Audrey Michellは27歳であった。1595年にイギリスエセックスデブデンでMary Bentleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1626年12月にイギリスエセックスデブデンで52歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスデブデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Sothy(이)가 1574년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 사프런 월든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas South(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Audrey Michell(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1595년 영국 에세스 데브든에서 Mary Bentley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1626년 12월 영국 에세스 데브든에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 데브든에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Sothy родился в 1574 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas South было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Audrey Michell, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Bentley в 1595 г. в местности дебден, эссекс, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер декабрь 1626 г. в местности дебден, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности дебден, эссекс, Англия.
